runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Python", "build_system": "pip", "package_manager": "pip", "build_commands": ["pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt", "pip install --no-cache-dir -e ."], "install_commands": [], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.12.13", "system_packages": []}, "repo_type": "web_service", "base_image": "python:3.12.13-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Uses a supported Python 3.12 image from the official Python family on Debian bookworm to align with Python code and dependencies.", "pitfalls": ["PyTorch wheels may not be available for Python 3.12 on Linux; installation could fail if a compatible Python version is not used.", "The project may rely on large binary packages (torch), which can lead to long install times and higher image size.", "Some assets referenced in setup_data may require additional build-time resources; ensure sufficient CPU/memory during build."], "notes": "The Dockerfile entrypoint runs the module as a web app (draggan.web). Installing the package in editable mode ensures the module is importable at runtime without requiring a separate installation step after building the image."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

WORKDIR /app

# Copy project
COPY . .

# Expose the Gradio/WebUI port (default 7860 for Drag GAN)
EXPOSE 7860

# Install Python dependencies
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Install the package in editable mode so it is importable at runtime
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -e .

# Run the web app by default
CMD ["bash", "start.sh"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

WORKDIR /app

# Copy project
COPY . .

# Expose the Gradio/WebUI port (default 7860 for DragGAN)
EXPOSE 7860

# Install Python dependencies
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Install the package in editable mode so it is importable at runtime
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -e .

# Run the web app
ENTRYPOINT [ "python", "-m", "draggan.web", "--ip", "0.0.0.0" ]
"}
